steps.title: Find BC using Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Triangle ABC has a right angle at C. Given $AB = \sqrt{34}$ and $AC = 3$:
$$AB^2 = AC^2 + BC^2$$
$$34 = 9 + BC^2$$
$$BC = 5$$

steps.title: Set up coordinates using the right angles
steps.content:
The right angles at $C$ and $E$ indicate perpendicularity to the base line $AE$. Place $A$ at the origin:
  • $A = (0, 0)$
  • $C = (3, 0)$ (since $AC = 3$)
  • $E = (24, 0)$ (since $AE = AC + CE = 3 + 21 = 24$)
  • $B = (3, 5)$ (since $BC \perp AE$ and $BC = 5$)
From the figure, points $A$, $B$, and $D$ are collinear.

steps.title: Find D using the line through A and B
steps.content:
The line through $A(0, 0)$ and $B(3, 5)$ has slope $\frac{5}{3}$:
$$y = \frac{5}{3}x$$

Point $D$ lies on this line and is directly above $E$ (at $x = 24$):
$$y = \frac{5}{3}(24) = 40$$

So $D = (24, 40)$. Triangle $ADE$ has base $AE = 24$ and perpendicular height 40:
$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2}(24)(40) = 480 \text{ square units}$$
